rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARUWN K. WESLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although Administrator did not disclose why cash on hand is $6,000 less than on the date of death and why the insurance proceeds are still on hand instead of having been deposited into an interest-bearing account. The time for filing creditor claims expired on 11/12/23, four months after Letters were issued, not 7/23/24. (There is no such thing as a "supplemental petition." A petition should be supplemented to provide additional information or an amendment should be filed to correct specific, substantive allegations or a fully amended petition should be filed if the entire petition needs to be amended. [CRC 7.3.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BILL WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/23/25. No waivers of account were attached to the petition or filed separately. Deadline for providing the examiner courtesy copies of any new filed papers is 6/9/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator was appointed on 10/23/23, not 12/1/23. Why are cash assets on hand still exactly the same values as on the date of death if all cash was invested in interest-bearing accounts? The account numbers are also the same account numbers shown on the I&A; the administrator has apparently not actually marshaled and deposited assets into estate accounts. What were the expenses Administrator paid for which he is seeking reimbursement?)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIE MAE TYLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner on 03/05/2025 & also posted as tentative ruling for 04/09/2025 hearing): 1) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to list petitioner's telephone number at item 2 of Acknowledgment of Receipt on page 2. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) Item 2 of the petition: Petitioner's name must be specified. 4) Item 2b: i) The name of the proposed personal representative must be specified. ii) At least one box must be checked. (3) must be checked for letters of administration. 5) Item 2d: At least one box must be checked. 6) Item 3c must be completed. 7) Item 3f(1) must be checked if the decedent died intestate. 8) Item 3g(2)(a) must be checked if petitioner is the decedent's child/heir. 9) Item 3g(4) must be unchecked. 10) Item 4 must be unchecked if the decedent died intestate. 11) Item 5a(1) or (2) must be checked. If (2) is checked, (a) or (b) must be checked. If (b) is checked, please provide the name and date of death of the decedent's spouse. 12) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 13) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 14) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. 15) Item 8: The relationship to the decedent must be specified for each person. In addition, a review of the DE-121 form filed on 03/28/2025 indicates that the following issues must be addressed: A) The county was not specified in the caption. B) The decedent's name must be specified in the caption (as part of document title) and at item 1. C) Items 2 and 3 must be completed. D) Items 2 and 3 of the proof of service must be completed. E) The proof of service must be signed by someone other than petitioner. F) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE YORITADA AND CHIYO WADA FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To 1)Remove The Trustee(Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Failure To Account). 2)Appoint Successor Trustee. 3)Surcharge Trustee. 4)Request Account
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/23/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) There are no attachments to the petition. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/9/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
